does: This paper introduces Ranked Choice Preference Optimization (RCPO), a unified framework for aligning large language models using richer forms of human feedback beyond pairwise comparisons. RCPO leverages maximum likelihood estimation to combine preference optimization with choice modeling, supporting both utility-based and rank-based models. The framework subsumes existing pairwise methods like DPO and SimPO as special cases and provides principled training objectives for multiway comparisons and top-k rankings. The authors instantiate RCPO with Multinomial Logit and Mallows-RMJ choice models, demonstrating on Llama-3-8B-Instruct, Gemma-2-9B-it, and Mistral-7B-Instruct that the approach consistently outperforms competitive baselines in both in-distribution and out-of-distribution evaluation settings.
connects: This work extends recent advances in preference-based alignment methods that moved beyond reinforcement learning approaches. It builds on Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) and related techniques like SimPO that learn directly from pairwise preference comparisons without explicit reward modeling. The paper recognizes limitations in the pairwise paradigm and draws from classical choice modeling literature, particularly multinomial logit and Mallows models, which have deep roots in econometrics and ranking theory. By unifying preference optimization with formal choice modeling through maximum likelihood estimation, RCPO addresses the broader problem of how to effectively leverage diverse forms of human feedback for alignment beyond the binary comparisons that dominate current practice.
enables: This framework opens several research directions for LLM alignment. It establishes a principled way to incorporate richer preference feedback formats, enabling future work to explore how organizations can collect and utilize more informative ranking annotations. The general RCPO framework suggests potential improvements by developing new choice models tailored to specific feedback characteristics or exploring hybrid approaches combining multiple modeling paradigms. The demonstrated effectiveness with ranked data points toward practical applications where preference data collection can be enhanced through ranking interfaces or evaluation schemes that naturally produce ordinal information. Future work could extend RCPO to handle partial rankings, incorporate uncertainty in annotations, or develop computationally efficient variants for scaling to larger models and datasets.
